purpose: A responsive multi-column container for master-detail navigation. Supports 1, 2, or 3 columns that expand/collapse based on user action and viewport width. The foundation of the Master-Detail floorplan.
whenToUse: Master-detail patterns (list on left + detail on right)
Drill-down navigation with visible parent context (master → detail → sub-detail)
When the user needs to keep the list visible while inspecting detail
Email-like flows: folder → message list → message detail
whenNotToUse: Single-object focus without a list — use DynamicPage
You need auxiliary content on the side — use DynamicSideContent
For search-first flows over big data — use List Report
doRules: Start in 1-column (begin) mode on load; expand as user selects items
Provide 'expand' and 'fullscreen' toggles in each column's header
On mobile, collapse to single-column with back navigation
Keep column widths responsive (25/40/35% is a common 3-col split)
dontRules: Do not use more than 3 columns — becomes unreadable
Do not use fixed column widths — must be responsive
Do not put unrelated content in separate columns — they should share hierarchy
Do not use for wizard flows — use Wizard instead

layoutGuidance.sizing: Full viewport height; column widths responsive
layoutGuidance.alignment: Columns fill height; each has its own scroll region

contentGuidance.contentRules: Each column contains a DynamicPage with its own title, header, and content
examples: begin: inbox list · mid: message detail · end: attached files

responsiveBehavior.M: 2 columns typical; end column may hide

requirements: Each column has landmark role (main / complementary)
Focus moves into newly opened column on selection
Back navigation announced by screen reader on mobile
Keyboard: Tab moves through columns in visual order
